briefdescription.para: Macro used internally by NANO_CPU_INT_REGISTER and NANO_CPU_INT_REGISTER_ASM.

detaileddescription.para: Not meant to be used explicitly by platform, driver or application code.

briefdescription.para: Connect a routine to an interrupt vector.

detaileddescription.para: This macro "connects" the specified routine, r, to the specified interrupt vector, v using the descriptor privilege level d. On the IA-32 architecture, an interrupt vector is a value from 0 to 255. This macro populates the special intList section with the address of the routine, the vector number and the descriptor privilege level. The genIdt tool then picks up this information and generates an actual IDT entry with this information properly encoded.

The d argument specifies the privilege level for the interrupt-gate descriptor; (hardware) interrupts and exceptions should specify a level of 0, whereas handlers for user-mode software generated interrupts should specify 3.
r
Routine to be connected
n
IRQ number
p
IRQ priority
v
Interrupt Vector
d
Descriptor Privilege Level

memberdef.name: ARCH_IRQ_CONNECT
memberdef.initializer:
{ \ __asm__ __volatile__( \ ".pushsection .intList
	" \ ".long %c[isr]_irq%c[irq]_stub
	" /* ISR_LIST.fnc */ \ ".long %c[irq]
	" /* ISR_LIST.irq */ \ ".long %c[priority]
	" /* ISR_LIST.priority */ \ ".long %c[vector]
	" /* ISR_LIST.vec */ \ ".long 0
	" /* ISR_LIST.dpl */ \ ".long 0
	" /* ISR_LIST.tss */ \ ".popsection
	" \ ".pushsection " IRQSTUBS_TEXT_SECTION "
	" \ ".global %c[isr]_irq%c[irq]_stub
	" \ "%c[isr]_irq%c[irq]_stub:
	" \ "endbr32
	" \ "pushl %[isr_param]
	" \ "pushl %[isr]
	" \ "jmp _interrupt_enter
	" \ ".popsection
	" \ : \ : [isr] "i" (isr_p), \ [isr_param] "i" (isr_param_p), \ [priority] "i" (priority_p), \ [vector] "i" _VECTOR_ARG(irq_p), \ [irq] "i" (irq_p)); \ z_irq_controller_irq_config(Z_IRQ_TO_INTERRUPT_VECTOR(irq_p), (irq_p), \ (flags_p)); \ }

memberdef.name: ARCH_ISR_DIRECT_DECLARE
memberdef.initializer: static inline int name##_body(void); \ __attribute__ ((interrupt)) void name(void *stack_frame) \ { \ ARG_UNUSED(stack_frame); \ int check_reschedule; \ ISR_DIRECT_HEADER(); \ check_reschedule = name##_body(); \ ISR_DIRECT_FOOTER(check_reschedule); \ } \ static inline int name##_body(void)

briefdescription.para: The NANO_SOFT_IRQ macro must be used as the value for the irq parameter to NANO_CPU_INT_REGISTER when connecting to an interrupt that does not correspond to any IRQ line (such as spurious vector or SW IRQ)

detaileddescription.para: This header contains the IA-32 portion of the X86 specific kernel interface (see include/zephyr/arch/x86/cpu.h).
